answer:
The condition \(\sin(x) < \frac{1}{2}\) on \([0,1]\) gives \(x \in [0, \frac{\pi}{6})\) since \(\pi/6 \approx 0.5236\).  
The set where \(\cos(x+y)\) is irrational has full measure because the set of rational values has measure zero.  
Thus the measure is the area of the strip: length in \(x\) is \(\pi/6\), length in \(y\) is 1, so measure \(\pi/6 \approx 0.5235987667\), which corresponds to option H.

\boxed{H}
